topic: Tabby AI Review 2026: Self-Hosted GitHub Copilot Alternative

competitors.title: Tabby: Self-Hosted GitHub Copilot Alternative | Local AI Master
key_points: Author tested Tabby for 3 months with a team of 5 on a single RTX 4090
Completion quality is 85-90% of Copilot for standard coding patterns
Setup took 20 minutes via Docker
Sub-200ms latency is usability threshold; above that, developers notice delay
25-35% acceptance rate (comparable to Copilot's public metrics)
ROI breakeven for 10 devs at 1.3 years; for 25 devs at just 6.4 months
RTX 4090 at 95 completions/sec supports ~20 concurrent active developers
Tabby does NOT include chat, agent mode, code explanation, or PR analysis — focused scope is an advantage
Cost table: 5-dev team pays $600/yr for Copilot vs $1,600 (RTX 4090 hardware), breakeven 2.7 years; 100-dev team $12,000/yr Copilot vs $4,500 hardware, breakeven 4.5 months

competitors.title: Best Local-First AI Coding Tools 2026: 14 Compared - Nimbalyst
key_points: Published April 20, 2026 — most comprehensive recent comparison of 14 local-first AI coding tools
Tabby categorized as 'Best for enterprise compliance' alongside Codeium Enterprise
Tabby is 'the clearest pick for teams that need an air-gapped or fully self-hosted AI coding assistant'
Other top competitors: Continue.dev, Cline, Aider, Roo Code, Kilo Code, Zed, Goose
All major competitors are Apache 2.0 licensed: Cline, Continue.dev, Aider, Tabby, Roo Code, Kilo Code, Goose
Tabby differentiates with built-in server (not Ollama), SSO, admin dashboard, multi-user management
Qwen3-Coder-Next, Llama 4 Scout, and DeepSeek V3.2 now match GPT-4 quality in early 2026
Recommendation: Enterprise/on-prem team → Tabby (open source, admin dashboard, SSO) or Codeium Enterprise

competitors.title: Best Copilot Alternatives for 2026 - OpenReplay Blog
key_points: Positions Tabby under 'Enterprise and Privacy-Focused Options' alongside Tabnine
Described as: 'open-source and self-hosted. Full offline deployment after setup. Less polished than commercial options, but offers complete data control'
Best for: 'Teams requiring air-gapped or fully self-hosted solutions'
Contrasts Tabby with agentic editors (Cursor, Windsurf) and CLI agents (Claude Code, Aider)
For non-regulated use cases, recommends Cursor or Claude Code over Tabby

competitors.title: Tabby - Copilot Alternatives
key_points: Strengths: self-contained architecture, compatible with CodeLlama/StarCoder/CodeGen, consumer-grade GPU support, adaptive caching for sub-second completions, OpenAPI interface
Weaknesses: requires infrastructure setup/maintenance, hardware requirements range from NVIDIA T4 (1B-3B models) to V100/A100 (7B-13B models), limited enterprise support vs commercial
Tabby Cloud: usage-based pricing, $20 monthly free credits, automatic billing above $10 threshold
When NOT to choose Tabby: if you prefer zero-infrastructure maintenance, lack GPU resources, or need SLA-backed enterprise support

competitors.title: 10 Smart GitHub Copilot Alternatives for Coding in 2026 | DigitalOcean
key_points: Lists Tabby among top 10 GitHub Copilot alternatives for 2026
Confirms Apache 2.0 open-source license — full rights to modify, deploy, and distribute
Highlights self-hosted server model as key architectural differentiator from all plugin-only alternatives
Notes repository context indexing as major completions quality differentiator

stats.fact: Tabby has 33,500+ GitHub stars and 1,700 forks as of May 2026
Tabby has 129 contributors, 3,694 commits, and 249 releases — latest stable v0.32.0 released January 25, 2026
Codebase is 92.9% Rust for maximum performance and memory safety
Tabby completion quality is 85-90% of GitHub Copilot for standard coding patterns, per 3-month team deployment study
25-35% completion acceptance rate in production — comparable to GitHub Copilot's publicly reported metrics
RTX 4090 delivers 95 StarCoder2-3B completions/sec, supporting ~20 concurrent active developers
ROI breakeven for 10-developer team: 1.3 years (RTX 4090 at $1,600 vs $1,200/year Copilot). For 25 developers: 6.4 months
GitHub Copilot has 4.7 million paid subscribers as of January 2026, up 75% year-over-year
92% of developers now use AI coding assistants daily as of early 2026
More than 70% of digital transformation leaders now prioritize data residency and auditability when evaluating AI platforms
EU AI Act high-risk system provisions become enforceable August 2026, strengthening self-hosted AI compliance demand
FedRAMP 20x Phase 3 set for wide-scale adoption H2 2026, formalizing federal AI deployment requirements
Original Show HN post (April 2023) received 627 points and 126 comments — strong developer community reception
Better Stack YouTube review (March 2026): 18,999 views, 313 likes — growing mainstream awareness
Apple M3 Max power draw: only 5W idle/40W active ($1-3/month electricity) vs RTX 4090 at 40W idle/300W active ($7-26/month)
GPU benchmark: RTX 3060 12GB delivers 45 completions/sec (StarCoder2-3B); RTX 5090 delivers 110 completions/sec

angles: The math behind Tabby vs GitHub Copilot: at what team size does self-hosting win financially?
Tabby is the only major open-source AI coding assistant with a built-in multi-user server (vs plugin-only tools like Cline/Continue.dev that rely on Ollama)
Compliance angle: HIPAA, SOC 2, GDPR, FedRAMP requirements are driving enterprise adoption of self-hosted AI coding tools in 2026
Pochi: Tabby's new agentic layer (December 2025) brings autonomous GitHub issue→PR creation — closing the gap with Copilot Coding Agent
Model flexibility is Tabby's hidden advantage: choose StarCoder2 (1B-15B), DeepSeek-Coder, CodeLlama, CodeGemma, Qwen2.5-Coder, or Mistral Code
The 'trust' differentiator: 'technically impossible to look' vs 'we promise not to look' — how self-hosting changes the compliance conversation
AMD GPU support (v0.23.0) and Apple Silicon support make Tabby viable without expensive NVIDIA hardware
Tabby's focused scope (code completion only, no chat/agent) as a deliberate design choice — why narrower is better for some teams
Repository indexing and LDAP authentication make Tabby a serious enterprise contender, not just a hobbyist tool
2026 local model quality inflection point: Qwen3-Coder-Next and DeepSeek V3.2 now match GPT-4 — making self-hosted viable for production
